species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 383 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 299; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 469; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 421.
species.distribution: S America. APULIT, LUZON: Rizal (photos), MANAMOC, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Introduced from Tropical America during the Spanish colonial times. Widely cultivated in the tropics for its nut, the cashew. Naturalized throughout the seasonal parts of the Philippines in settled areas at low and medium elevation, often on sandy soils in villages near seacoasts. Cashew.

APULIT, BAGUAN, BALABAC, BASILAN, BOHOL, BUSUANGA, CAGAYAN DE SULU, CEBU, CULION, DALUPIRI, DANJUGAN (photos), DINAGAT, GUIMARAS, HOMONHON, LAGEN, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Apayao (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MALAMAWI, MARINDUQUE, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Misamis, Surigao, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, PONEAS, SAMAR, SEMIRARA, SIARGAO, SIBUTU, SIBUYAN, TAWI-TAWI, TICAO, TINAGO.

species.citation: K.Schum. , Nachtr. Fl. Schutzgeb. Südsee (1905) 300 ; Buhay et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 3085; Type: from New Guinea. = Campnosperma philippinensis Merr. , PJS 60 (1936) 31; Type: Wenzel 3199, Mindanao: Surigao, near Placer, 28-Mar-1928.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls. BILIRAN, DINAGAT (photos), MINDANAO: Agusan del Sur (photos), Surigao del Sur, SAMAR.
species.notes: Note by James V. LaFrankie (pers. comm., 9-Apr-14): "Ding Hou considered that name [Buchanania heterophylla K.Schum.] a synonym of B. amboinensis. Both are still widely used in New Guinea, but heterophylla seems always to be used for plants with really big thick-stalked leaves where the blade comes broadly right to the twig. B. amboinensis is much more like insignis.".

BASILAN, BILIRAN, BUCAS GRANDE, CATANDUANES, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Camarines, Camarines Norte, Ifugao, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Forests. Occasionally on limestone. Note by P.B. Pelser (6-Aug-19): Buchanania nitida is here considered a synonym of Buchanania insignis following LaFrankie (pers. comm. 9-Apr-14). 0-450m.

species.citation: Engl. , Monogr. Phan. 4 (1883) 185 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 466; Ding Hou, Blumea 24 (1978) 4; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 413; Type: Cuming 1125 (K, holo; L, iso), Luzon: Albay.

species.notes: In seasonal parts of the archipelago, on dry slopes, thickets and secondary forests. 0-400m.

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1851) 184, as 'sessifolia'.

species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser & J.P. Collantes (13-Sep-20): Ridsdale 1905 (L) and Romero et al. PPI 15638 (L) possibly represent this species in the Philippines.

species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser & J.P. Collantes (13-Aug-20): PPI 8620 Barbon, Alvarez & Fuentes (L) possibly represents this species in the Philippines.

D. edule, D. lamiyo, & D. brachyphyllum; Batuyong et al., Biodiversitas 21 (2020) 3973; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1193; ≡ Paliurus dao Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 174. = Dracontomelon sylvestre Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1850) 231 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 472. = Dracontomelon mangiferum Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1850) 231.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, GUIMARAS (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Cotabato, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Norte, Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: In Luzon from Cagayan and Ilocos Norte to Sorsogon. Common in primary and secondary forests. In high rainfall areas, although sometimes with a short dry season. 0-500(-1000)m. Common name: Dao.

species.citation: (Blanco) Skeels , Bull. Bur. Pl. Industr. U.S.D.A. 261 (1912) 52 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 471; ≡ Paliurus edulis Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 173.
species.distribution: CEBU, LUZON: Bataan, Batangas, Bulacan, Camarines, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Cotabato, PALAWAN, SAMAR, TABLAS.
species.notes: Common in lowland primary forests, along riverbanks and on hills. This species is widely considered a synonym of Dracontomelon dao, but is considered distinct in the Philippines. It is mostly a smaller tree with larger fruits, and lacks the great sweeping buttresses of Dracontomelon dao. Common name: Lamio.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 200 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 300; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 468; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 427.
species.distribution: China, India, Myanmar, Thailand. LUZON: Aurora (photos), Batangas (photos), Cagayan (photos), Mountain Province (photos), Zambales (photos).
species.notes: Probably a native of Indo-Myanmar region and widely cultivated throughout the tropics of both hemispheres. Generally planted as a village tree and cultivated commercially in Peninsular Malaysia, Java and the Philippines. Seemingly indigenous or naturalized in India, Myanmar, Thailand, Indochina and some islands of West Malesia. Generally cultivated below 500m.

species.citation: Kosterm. , Reinwardtia 7 (1965) 20.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. PALAWAN.
species.notes: John Edward Matulina Villasis (2-Feb-25): Reported by locals to grow in the wild, chiefly in lowland dipterocarp forests, but needs verification as this species might be introduced for cultivation, perhaps dispersed by early humans. Rare within its native range, except the west coast plains of Sabah, thus possibly extending to S Palawan.

C.B.Rob. , PJS 6c (1911) 340 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 475. = Semecarpus surigaensis Merr. , PJS 17 (1921) 272 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 475.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Sur (photos), Surigao, Surigao del Norte (photos), SAMAR.
species.notes: Forests on dryland, along streams. Low elevation.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 286 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 475; Ding Hou, Blumea 24 (1978) 36; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 510. = Semecarpus obtusatus Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1752, as 'obtusata' ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 475; Type: Elmer 13003, Palawan: Puerto Princesa, Mt Pulgar, Iwahig River, Apr-1911.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Lowland primary forests, along streambank. 0-225m. Fl. Feb, fr. May.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 30 (1926) 407 ; Ding Hou, Blumea 24 (1978) 37; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 507.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Isabela, SAMAR.
species.notes: Riverbank thickets. 150m.

species.citation: Perkins , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 29, as 'trachyphylla' ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 508; ≡ Oncocarpus trachyphyllus (Perkins) Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 476, as 'trachyphylla'; Type: Warburg 11796, Luzon: Malunu. = Semecarpus macrothyrsus Perkins , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 26, as 'macrothyrsa' ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 474; Type: Merrill 2447, Luzon: Tayabas, Malicboi.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan, Davao, SAMAR.
species.notes: Primary lowland forests.

species.citation: (Elmer) Steenis , PJS 91 (1962) 508, nom. illeg., non Moon ex Thwaites ; Ding Hou, Blumea 24 (1978) 37; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 518; ≡ Oncocarpus obovatus (Elmer) Merr. , PJS 14 (1919) 413 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 476; ≡ Dichapetalum obovatum Elmer , LPB 2 (1908) 483.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines.
species.notes: It cannot be properly placed from the descriptions and no material has been traced. Ding Hou (1978): Described from a specimen with one immature fruit from Mt Banahao, Lucban, Tayabas, Luzon (Elmer 7931), later supplemented with a male flowered collection from the type locality (Quisumbing 1346).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 24 November 201

species.citation: (Elmer) Kosterm. , New Crit. Malaysian Pl. 3 (1955) 1 ; Mitchell & Daly, PhytoKeys 55 (2015) 4; ≡ Spondias philippinensis (Elmer) Airy Shaw & Forman , Kew Bull. 21 (1967) 15 ; Ding Hou, Blumea 24 (1978) 38; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 485; ≡ Phlebochiton philippinensis (Elmer) Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 472, as 'philippinense'; ≡ Pegia philippinensis Elmer , LPB 8 (1919) 3100.
species.distribution: Borneo, New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra. LEYTE, LUZON: Cavite, Laguna, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, MINDORO, SAMAR.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 May 2026

species.citation: Parkinson , J. Voy. South Seas (1773) 39 ; Mitchell & Daly, PhytoKeys 55 (2015) 22. = Spondias cytherea Sonn. , Voy. Indes Orient. (Sonnerat), 1st Ed. 3 (1782) 242 ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 481.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Solomon Isls.
species.notes: Cultivated in the Philippines and other tropical countries. Lowlands.

species.citation: Kosterm. , Quart. J. Taiwan Mus. 34 (1981) 108 ; Soejarto et al., Prelim. Checklist Fl. Pl. Palawan (1995).
species.distribution: Cambodia, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Thailand. LAGEN, LUZON: Camarines Sur, Laguna, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY.
species.notes: Known from Lagen Isl in Palawan from SMHI 825 (L, PNH).

species.citation: (L.f.) Kurz , Prelim. Rep. Forest Pegu App. A (1875) 44 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 470; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 483; ≡ Mangifera pinnata L.f. , Suppl. Pl. (1786) 156.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Thailand, Vietnam. APULIT, CATANDUANES, CORON, CULION, LUZON: Apayao (photos), Bataan, Cagayan, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Cotabato, Davao, Zamboanga, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Indo-Malesia, especially in Java and the Philippines, but difficult to ascertain where it is precisely native because of its wide cultivation and tendency to naturalize. Besides in the cultivated state, found in both primary and secondary forests, clearings, and in dry areas, sometimes on limestone, typically in the lowlands. 0-500(-900)m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 371 ; Mitchell & Daly, PhytoKeys 55 (2015) 34; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1978) 484.
species.distribution: Neotropics.
species.notes: Widely cultivated in Malesia.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1762) 613 ; Mitchell & Daly, PhytoKeys 55 (2015) 41; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 301; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 471. = Spondias negrosensis Kosterm. , Kedondong, Ambarella, Amra (1991) 28; Type: Merrill 639, Luzon: Rizal, Pasay, Apr-1913 (US, iso).
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON.
species.notes: Cultivated throughout the tropics. Cultivated, not naturalized.
